ops.title: cc-guides auto-cuts a tagged release on every main push; AGENTS.md § Releases (tag-driven) is stale
ops.body:
The cc-guides Release workflow auto-tags and releases on **every push to main** — observed 2026-07-19: two consecutive pushes auto-became v0.1.37 and v0.1.38 (goreleaser + Homebrew tap push, ~6.5 min each), with no manual `git tag` involved; a manual tag attempt failed with "already exists". AGENTS.md § Releases still describes the old tag-driven flow ("Tagging v* triggers release.yml") — treat that section as stale until someone re-renders it from updated fragments.

**Why:** Prevents wasted work planning manual release tags, and means every main push is a public release — batch commits accordingly and don't push half-done work to main.

**How to apply:** To release, just land the commit on main and watch the Release run (`ccx vcs ship` covers it). Don't pre-allocate version numbers in commit messages or plans — the workflow assigns the next patch version per push.
